raw_claim: For all real c > 0 and γ > 0 with γ ≠ 1, -c * (-γ * c⁻¹) = γ
For a consumer with income m and prices p₁, p₂ spending all income: p₁ * x₁ + p₂ * x₂ = m
For all real x > 0: x * x⁻¹ = 1
For all real α with 0 < α < 1 and K > 0: α * K * K⁻¹ = α
For all real a b with b ≠ 0: a / b * b = a
For all real α > 0 and e > 0: -(-α * e) / e = α
For all real x > 0 and y > 0: x * y > 0
The derivative of x^α with respect to x is α * x^(α-1) for x > 0
A continuous function on [a,b] attains its maximum
If f is differentiable and f'(x₀) = 0 and f''(x₀) < 0, then x₀ is a local maximum
For a Cobb-Douglas production function f(K,L) = A·K^α·L^(1-α), the output elasticity w.r.t. capital is α
The marginal rate of substitution equals the price ratio at an interior optimum
Under CRRA utility u(c) = c^(1-γ)/(1-γ), the relative risk aversion equals γ
CARA utility u(c) = -exp(-αc)/α has constant absolute risk aversion equal to α
A monotone sequence bounded above converges
A contraction mapping on a nonempty complete metric space has a unique fixed point
A strictly concave function on a compact convex set attains a unique maximum
If f is twice continuously differentiable and the Hessian is negative definite at x, then f is strictly concave near x
A continuous function on a nonempty compact set attains its minimum
Under Inada conditions, the Solow model has a unique positive steady state
A concave function on a convex set is continuous on the interior
The composition of two continuous functions is continuous
A closed subset of a compact space is compact
If f is convex and differentiable, then f(y) ≥ f(x) + f'(x)(y-x) for all x,y
A monotone operator on a complete lattice has a least fixed point
Nash equilibrium exists in all finite games
A competitive Walrasian equilibrium exists under standard assumptions
The second welfare theorem holds with convex preferences
Arrow's impossibility theorem: no social welfare function satisfies all four axioms
The VCG mechanism is strategyproof
Every sub-game perfect equilibrium is a Nash equilibrium
